rationale: The citing paper uses Jain and Srivastava (2000) for a specific empirical claim, not just as a general literature-list mention. In the price-matching-with-loyalty application, it cites that study as evidence that consumers interpret price-matching guarantees as low-price signals. That cited finding is then used to justify the goodwill reward/penalty structure in the game the authors discuss, so it meaningfully informs the setup of the application.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the cited paper's consumer-inference insight (price-matching guarantees signal lower prices) to motivate assumptions in its price-matching game application. The cited evidence supports how the review maps behavioral insights into a marketing competition model.
